mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 70 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1.0 percent indicates exhausted monetary stimulus, removing the primary driver of equity multiples and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a negative or zero mean 20-day forward return across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of observations, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, an accelerating industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and supply-side normalization, generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in recovery regimes fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.
